description: Implement a statistics library. Tasks 1-8 form a strict sequential dependency chain — each function must be completed before the next can begin because each one imports from the previous. Tasks 9-10 are fully independent and can be done at any time.

tasks.title: Implement add function
tasks.description: Create add.py containing add(a: float, b: float) -> float. Returns a + b. Include a one-line docstring. No dependencies — this is the foundation of the entire chain.

tasks.title: Implement safe_add function
tasks.description: Create safe_add.py containing safe_add(a: float, b: float) -> float. Validates that both a and b are int or float (raise TypeError if not), then delegates to add(a, b) imported from add.py. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-1 (add) to be completed first — this function imports from add.py.

tasks.title: Implement sum_list function
tasks.description: Create sum_list.py containing sum_list(lst: list) -> float. Iterates over lst and accumulates using safe_add(total, x) imported from safe_add.py. Returns 0.0 for an empty list. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-2 (safe_add) to be completed first — this function imports from safe_add.py.

tasks.title: Implement mean function
tasks.description: Create mean.py containing mean(lst: list) -> float. Computes the arithmetic mean by calling sum_list(lst) imported from sum_list.py and dividing by len(lst). Raise ValueError if lst is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-3 (sum_list) to be completed first — this function imports from sum_list.py.

tasks.title: Implement variance function
tasks.description: Create variance.py containing variance(lst: list) -> float. Computes population variance as sum((x - m)**2 for x in lst) / len(lst) where m = mean(lst) imported from mean.py. Raise ValueError if lst is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-4 (mean) to be completed first — this function imports from mean.py.

tasks.title: Implement std_dev function
tasks.description: Create std_dev.py containing std_dev(lst: list) -> float. Computes population standard deviation as variance(lst) ** 0.5, calling variance imported from variance.py. Raise ValueError if lst is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-5 (variance) to be completed first — this function imports from variance.py.

tasks.title: Implement z_score function
tasks.description: Create z_score.py containing z_score(x: float, lst: list) -> float. Computes the z-score of x relative to lst as (x - mean(lst)) / std_dev(lst). Import mean from mean.py and std_dev from std_dev.py. Raise ValueError if std_dev is zero. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-4 (mean) AND task-6 (std_dev) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement normalize function
tasks.description: Create normalize.py containing normalize(lst: list) -> list. Returns a new list where each element x is replaced by z_score(x, lst) imported from z_score.py. Raise ValueError if lst is empty or all values are identical. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-7 (z_score) to be completed first — this function imports from z_score.py.

tasks.title: Implement abs_val function
tasks.description: Create abs_val.py containing abs_val(x: float) -> float. Returns the absolute value of x without using the built-in abs(). Include a one-line docstring. No dependencies on other tasks.

tasks.title: Implement is_even function
tasks.description: Create is_even.py containing is_even(n: int) -> bool. Returns True if n is even, False otherwise. Include a one-line docstring. No dependencies on other tasks.

success_criteria: All 10 functions implemented. Tasks 1-8 form a sequential dependency chain; tasks 9-10 are independent.
